idea.hook: What's the ULTIMATE cosmic road trip?
facts: Our entire Milky Way galaxy, along with its galactic neighbors, is being gravitationally pulled.
This pull comes from a mysterious region called the Great Attractor, located roughly 250 million light-years away.
Even if you could travel at the universe's maximum speed — the speed of light (299,792,458 m/s)...
idea.payoff: ...it would STILL take you 250 MILLION years to reach it! That's longer than dinosaurs walked the Earth.
idea.title: Cosmic Road Trip: Great Attractor! 🤯🌌

scenes.text: What's the ULTIMATE cosmic road trip?
scenes.visual: A vast, deep space background filled with distant galaxies and nebulae, slowly zooming out to reveal more cosmic expanse.

scenes.text: Our Milky Way & neighbors are being gravitationally pulled.
scenes.visual: Stylized representation of the Milky Way and several surrounding galaxies, with subtle arrows indicating movement towards an off-screen point.

scenes.text: This pull comes from the Great Attractor, 250 million light-years away.
scenes.visual: An abstract, dense cosmic region (the Great Attractor) appears, surrounded by a faint web of gravitational distortion. A line with '250 MILLION LIGHT-YEARS' points from a galactic cluster to it.
A sleek, futuristic starship or light beam streaks across a starry void, with a graphic displaying 'SPEED OF LIGHT' and its value.

scenes.text: ...it would STILL take 250 MILLION years to reach! Longer than dinosaurs walked Earth.
scenes.visual: A swirling cosmic vortex representing the Great Attractor dominates the screen. A timeline graphic compares '250 MILLION YEARS' to 'Dinosaurs (180 MY)' with a fading dinosaur silhouette.
